title: Reconstruct elapsed-time gaps and pit-exit traffic pressure
summary: F1 undercut and cover decisions depend on gaps to nearby cars and whether a driver would rejoin in traffic, but the current features mostly use Position without time gaps.
rationale: F1 undercut and cover decisions depend on gaps to nearby cars and whether a driver would rejoin in traffic, but the current features mostly use Position without time gaps. LapTime is enough to approximate cumulative race time per driver and derive gap_ahead, gap_behind, local pack density, and clean-air probability.
implementation_hint: Within each Race_Year, Driver sequence, cumulative-sum LapTime to ElapsedRaceTime. For each lap, rank drivers by elapsed time and compute nearest gaps, number of cars within 2/5/10/20 seconds, and interactions with Position, TyreLife, and LapsRemaining_Est. Use only current and prior lap times.
expected_effect: Can capture undercut/overcut timing beyond tyre-age thresholds, especially around pit windows where AUC errors are concentrated.
risk: LapTime outliers from pit laps, safety car, or testing runs can distort elapsed gaps; winsorize and add missing/outlier flags.
